product_overview: Ecoza Max is a high-strength neem-based botanical insecticide formulated as an emulsifiable concentrate containing 3% Azadirachtin extracted from Azadirachta indica seed kernels. Due to its higher concentration of the active ingredient, it delivers stronger efficacy at lower application dosages, making it suitable for intensive pest management programs and large-scale agricultural applications while supporting environmentally responsible pest control practices.
key_product_highlights: Protects Crops from the Damaging Immature and Adult Stage Insects.
Effective on a Wide Range of Target Pests and Crops.
Completely Restricts Growth by Disrupting the Hormonal Control of Molting.
Help Prevent or Delay Resistance in Pests.
Easy to Handle and Compatible with Other Pesticides.
Great Tool to Supplement the existing IPM programs.

mechanism_of_action.description: Azadirachtin functions primarily as a feeding deterrent and insect growth regulator. It disrupts ecdysone- and juvenile-hormone-mediated endocrine signaling, interferes with molting and metamorphosis, suppresses larval feeding, and reduces fecundity, oviposition, and egg viability in exposed pest populations.

dilution_dosage_per_acre.crop_stage: Early infestation or preventive canopy spray
dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Foliar spray with thorough canopy coverage
Foliar spray targeting the undersides of leaves

application_repeatability.application_timing: At first appearance of nymphs or early larval stages
application_repeatability.recommendation: Use the lower dose as a preventive treatment and the upper dose under rising pest pressure.

application_repeatability.application_timing: During active flush or continuous pest emergence
application_repeatability.recommendation: Maintain full coverage of tender foliage and the lower leaf surface for better suppression.

shelf_life_statement: Typical shelf life: 24 months in the unopened original container under cool, dry, shaded storage.
compatibility_statement: Compatible with most neutral-pH spray inputs; avoid strongly alkaline mixes and confirm with a jar test.

problem_solution.problem: Sucking pests are causing curling, chlorosis, and honeydew deposition.
problem_solution.solution: Apply early to deter feeding and suppress immature development before populations peak.

problem_solution.problem: Repeated infestations are occurring after short-residual contact sprays.
problem_solution.solution: Use sequential applications to interrupt molting and reduce oviposition across overlapping generations.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: High-strength azadirachtin EC positioned for lower-dose foliar programs where strong active loading and quick emulsification are preferred.

product_overview: Ecoza Ace is a neem-based botanical insecticide formulated as an emulsifiable concentrate containing 1.2% Azadirachtin derived from Azadirachta indica seed kernels. It acts primarily as an antifeedant, insect growth regulator, and repellent, interfering with the feeding, molting, and reproduction of insect pests. It is commonly used for regular pest management programs in agricultural and horticultural crops, providing effective control while maintaining a low environmental impact.
key_product_highlights: Broad Spectrum Biopesticide
Multiple modes of Action - IGR, Antifeedant, Repellent
IPM friendly
Compatible with Beneficials
Low Residue & better UV Stability.

product_overview: Ecoza Pro is a neem-based botanical insecticide formulated as an Emulsifiable Concentrate (EC) containing 0.3% Azadirachtin as the active ingredient derived from neem seed kernels. It acts primarily as an antifeedant, insect growth regulator, and repellent, disrupting the feeding, molting, and reproductive cycles of insect pests. Ecoza Pro is widely used for the management of sucking and chewing pests in agricultural and horticultural crops and is valued for its biodegradability, low toxicity to non-target organisms, and compatibility with Integrated Pest Management (IPM) and organic farming systems.
key_product_highlights: Broad-Spectrum Biopesticide.
Over 100 biologically active limonoids.
Excellent UV stability for prolonged efficacy.
High emulsification for uniform active ingredient dispersion.
Great for foliar & systemic applications.
Superior activity on sucking & mining pests.

It disrupts ecdysone- and juvenile-hormone-mediated endocrine signaling, interferes with molting and metamorphosis, suppresses larval feeding, and reduces fecundity, oviposition, and egg viability in exposed pest populations.

product_overview: Ecoza Rix is a neem-derived botanical insecticide formulated as a Water Soluble Powder (WP) containing 1.2% Azadirachtin extracted from neem seed kernels. The formulation is designed to readily dissolve in water, ensuring uniform dispersion and ease of application. It functions primarily as an insect growth regulator (IGR), antifeedant, and oviposition deterrent, disrupting the hormonal balance of insect pests and thereby inhibiting their feeding, molting, and reproduction. Ecoza Rix is suitable for use in sustainable crop protection programs, integrated pest management (IPM), and environmentally responsible agricultural practices.
key_product_highlights: Multiple modes of action: antifeedant, repellent, sterilant, insect growth regulator, and oviposition deterrent.
Minimal impact on natural predators and pollinators.
Suitable IPM and organic programs.
No phytotoxicity; Biodegradable, non-persistent & Non toxic residue.
Controls a wide range of pests at multiple life stages.

product_overview: MargoShine is a natural, broad-spectrum botanical insecticide, acaricide, and fungicide derived from the seeds of the Neem tree (Azadirachta indica). It acts as an antifeedant, repellent, oviposition deterrent, and insect growth regulator. MargoShine interferes with the hormonal balance of pests, preventing molting, feeding, and reproduction. Safe for beneficial insects, it provides effective pest management for sustainable and organic farming systems.
key_product_highlights: 100% natural and biodegradable insecticide.
Multi-mode action ensures long-lasting control.
Safe for beneficial insects like honeybees and pollinators.
No chemical residues on crops.
Enhances overall plant health and vigor.
Ideal for IPM and organic farming systems.

mechanism_of_action.description: Neem oil coats insect and egg surfaces, disrupting spiracular respiration and cuticular integrity, while associated neem limonoids act as feeding deterrents and mild insect growth regulators. Exposure interferes with endocrine control of molting, lowers feeding intensity, and suppresses oviposition and egg hatch in treated populations.

dilution_dosage_per_acre.crop_stage: Egg, nymph, and soft-bodied pest stage
dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Foliar spray with complete surface wetting
Foliar spray targeting hidden colonies and lower leaf surfaces

application_repeatability.application_timing: At first detection of eggs, nymphs, or small larvae
application_repeatability.recommendation: Apply during morning or late afternoon to improve spreading and reduce heat stress on the crop.

application_repeatability.application_timing: During persistent soft-bodied pest pressure
application_repeatability.recommendation: Reapply after heavy wash-off and maintain coverage on the lower leaf surface.

strain_or_active_strength: Neem oil standardized to 70% total oil phase.
shelf_life_statement: Typical shelf life: 24 months in the unopened original container under cool, dry, shaded storage.
compatibility_statement: Compatible with most neutral-pH spray inputs; avoid strongly alkaline mixes and confirm with a jar test.

problem_solution.problem: Soft-bodied pests and eggs are escaping contact from conventional sprays.
problem_solution.solution: Use neem oil with high coverage to coat colonies, suppress hatch, and reduce feeding.

problem_solution.problem: Pest pressure rebounds quickly in humid crop canopies.
problem_solution.solution: Repeat short-interval applications to suppress successive egg and nymphal flushes.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: High-load neem oil EC positioned for strong contact coverage, ovicidal support, and limonoid-mediated feeding suppression.

product_overview: MargoRix is a water-soluble neem oil formulation developed for the broad-spectrum management of sucking pests and soft-bodied insects in agricultural and horticultural crops. It functions as an antifeedant, repellent, insect growth regulator, and contact suppressant, disrupting pest feeding and development. The formulation is designed to readily dissolve in water, ensuring easy tank mixing, uniform spray dispersion, and reliable field performance. MargoRix offers convenient handling and consistent application under both open-field and greenhouse cultivation systems, supporting sustainable and environmentally responsible crop protection programs.
key_product_highlights: Broad-spectrum Biopesticide, Biofungicide, Bioacaricide.
Multi mode of action repellent, antifeedent, growth regulator, ovicidal, fungistatic.
Suitable for organic farming and safe for beneficial insects.
It mixes easily in water & forms a stable solution.
Zero-residue, environmental safe & ideal for IPM.

mechanism_of_action.description: Neem oil coats insect and egg surfaces, disrupting spiracular respiration and cuticular integrity, while associated neem limonoids act as feeding deterrents and mild insect growth regulators. Exposure interferes with endocrine control of molting, lowers feeding intensity, and suppresses oviposition and egg hatch in treated populations.

product_overview: K-Guard is a 100% natural botanical bio-acaricide derived from Pongamia glabra (Karanja). It is enriched with karanjin, a naturally occurring furano-flavonoid known for its strong acaricidal, insecticidal, and antimicrobial properties. K-Guard delivers both preventive and curative protection, effectively managing mites and a wide range of insect pests. The formulation supports sustainable crop protection programs, offering broad-spectrum pest control while maintaining a low environmental impact.
key_product_highlights: 100% natural, biodegradable, and eco-friendly.
Safe for beneficial insects, pollinators, and soil microbes.
Exhibits insecticidal, repellent, and therapeutic properties.
Biopesticide, Miticide, Bactericide, Acaricide, suitable for organic farming.
Enhances plant health and vigor by reducing pest pressure.

mechanism_of_action.description: Karanjin acts as a feeding deterrent and contact-active botanical toxicant. It interferes with digestive physiology and insect hormonal regulation in immature stages, reduces larval growth and feeding, and suppresses adult fecundity and oviposition, thereby slowing population build-up.

dilution_dosage_per_acre.crop_stage: Early sucking or chewing pest infestation
dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Foliar spray with full canopy coverage
Foliar spray focusing on tender plant parts

application_repeatability.application_timing: At the onset of pest colonization
application_repeatability.recommendation: Use preventively or at low pest density for better population suppression.

application_repeatability.application_timing: During continuous pest emergence
application_repeatability.recommendation: Ensure coverage of new flush and actively feeding zones of the canopy.

shelf_life_statement: Typical shelf life: 24 months in the unopened original container under cool, dry, shaded storage.
compatibility_statement: Compatible with most neutral-pH spray inputs; avoid strongly alkaline mixes and confirm with a jar test.

problem_solution.problem: Chewing and sucking pests are establishing in new crop flush.
problem_solution.solution: Apply early to deter feeding and reduce settlement on tender foliage.

problem_solution.problem: Low-level infestations are expanding between crop scouting intervals.
problem_solution.solution: Use repeat applications to suppress oviposition and immature development over time.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Karanjin EC positioned for early intervention where a pongamia-derived antifeedant and contact botanical is required.

product_overview: K-Rix is a water-soluble powder (WP) formulation of karanja oil developed for the effective management of sucking pests and soft-bodied insects in agricultural and horticultural crops. It functions as an antifeedant, repellent, insect growth regulator, and contact suppressant, disrupting pest feeding and development while helping to reduce pest populations. The formulation is designed to dissolve readily in water, ensuring easy tank mixing, uniform spray coverage, and consistent performance in both field and greenhouse cultivation systems.
key_product_highlights: Dual action against insect pests and fungal pathogens.
Water-soluble and easy to mix for uniform spray coverage.
Non-toxic, residue-free, and safe for beneficial organisms.
Supports sustainable and organic crop production systems.

mechanism_of_action.description: Karanjin acts as a feeding deterrent and contact-active botanical toxicant. It interferes with digestive physiology and insect hormonal regulation in immature stages, reduces larval growth and feeding, and suppresses adult fecundity and oviposition, thereby slowing population build-up.

product_overview: Spindura Plus is a highly effective, broad-spectrum biological insecticide produced through the fermentation of the naturally occurring soil bacterium Saccharopolyspora spinosa. It acts on the nervous system of insect pests, causing rapid feeding cessation and eventual mortality. The product is highly effective against resistant and difficult-to-control insect populations in agricultural and horticultural crops.
key_product_highlights: Rapid knockdown and extended residual effect.
Novel mode of action targets nervous system, often effective where resistance to other insecticides has developed.
Safe for beneficial insects when used as directed, making it compatible with integrated pest management (IPM) strategies.
Minimal residue, safe for use close to harvest.

mechanism_of_action.description: Spinosad acts primarily on insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and secondarily on GABA-gated chloride channels, causing neuromuscular hyperexcitation, paralysis, and death. Treated larvae rapidly cease feeding, population growth is curtailed, and surviving adults often show reduced oviposition and lower egg viability.

dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Foliar spray with fine droplet coverage
Foliar spray targeting feeding sites and concealed plant parts

application_repeatability.application_timing: At first appearance of small larvae or thrips hotspots
application_repeatability.recommendation: Target early stages for better intake and reduced crop damage.

application_repeatability.application_timing: During continuous egg hatch or larval emergence
application_repeatability.frequency: Limit sequential use within rotation programs
application_repeatability.recommendation: Alternate with other compatible modes of action to manage resistance selection.

shelf_life_statement: Typical shelf life: 24 months in the unopened original container under cool, dry, shaded storage.
compatibility_statement: Compatible with most neutral-pH spray inputs; avoid strongly alkaline mixes and confirm with a jar test.

problem_solution.problem: Larvae are causing fresh feeding holes and rapid canopy loss.
problem_solution.solution: Apply at early larval stages to induce quick feeding cessation and suppress further injury.

problem_solution.problem: Thrips pressure persists despite contact-only sprays.
problem_solution.solution: Use directed applications on feeding sites and rotate with other tools in the spray program.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: High-load SC positioned for low-dose foliar use against larvae and thrips in high-value crop programs.

product_overview: Spindura Rix is a naturally derived bio-insecticide obtained through the fermentation of Saccharopolyspora spinosa. It provides rapid knockdown and long-lasting control of a wide range of lepidopteran pests, thrips, and leaf miners. Spinosad acts both by ingestion and contact, targeting the insect's nervous system while remaining safe for beneficial insects and the environment. It is ideal for use in organic and integrated pest management (IPM) systems.
key_product_highlights: Fast knockdown action and long residual control.
Broad-spectrum activity against chewing and sucking pests.
Derived from natural fermentation – safe for beneficial insects.
Suitable for organic and IPM cultivation.
Environmentally safe and leaves no harmful residues.

mechanism_of_action.description: Spinosad acts primarily on insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and secondarily on GABA-gated chloride channels, causing neuromuscular hyperexcitation, paralysis, and death. Treated larvae rapidly cease feeding, population growth is curtailed, and surviving adults often show reduced oviposition and lower egg viability.

product_overview: Spindura Pro is a biological insecticide produced through the aerobic fermentation of the soil actinomycete Saccharopolyspora spinosa. It provides effective control against a broad spectrum of chewing and sucking insect pests by targeting their nervous system, leading to rapid feeding cessation and pest mortality. The formulation ensures high efficacy even at low application rates, delivering reliable pest management in a wide range of crops. Due to its selective mode of action and low environmental impact, it has minimal effects on beneficial insects.
key_product_highlights: Fast knockdown with long residual activity.
Effective at low doses, providing broad-spectrum pest control.
Derived from natural fermentation — safe for beneficial insects and pollinators.
No chemical residues — ideal for residue-free farming.
Suitable for both organic and IPM programs.

mechanism_of_action.description: Spinosad acts primarily on insect nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and secondarily on GABA-gated chloride channels, causing neuromuscular hyperexcitation, paralysis, and death. Treated larvae rapidly cease feeding, population growth is curtailed, and surviving adults often show reduced oviposition and lower egg viability.

product_overview: MargoSpin is a synergistic bio-insecticide combining two natural actives—Neem Oil and Spinosad derived from Saccharopolyspora spinosa. Neem oil works as a repellent, antifeedant, and insect growth regulator, disrupting pest feeding and development. Spinosad targets the insect nervous system, leading to rapid feeding cessation and effective pest control. The combination provides broad-spectrum management of chewing and sucking pests in agricultural crops. This dual-action formulation enhances pest control efficacy, helps delay resistance development, and supports sustainable crop protection practices.
key_product_highlights: Combines two natural insecticides for broad-spectrum control.
Fast knockdown action with long residual effect.
Repels and kills pests through multiple biological mechanisms.
Safe for pollinators, beneficial insects, and the environment.
100% biodegradable and residue-free — ideal for organic farming.

mechanism_of_action.description: The neem oil fraction contributes contact smothering, feeding deterrence, and endocrine disruption that interferes with molting and oviposition, while spinosad acts on nicotinic acetylcholine receptors to induce rapid neuromuscular dysfunction. Together, the formulation suppresses feeding, development, reproduction, and overall pest survival across mixed infestations.

dilution_dosage_per_acre.crop_stage: Early mixed infestation of sucking pests and larvae
dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Foliar spray with complete canopy coverage
Foliar spray targeting both lower leaf surfaces and feeding zones

application_repeatability.application_timing: At first mixed infestation of nymphs, thrips, or small larvae
application_repeatability.recommendation: Use early before canopy infestation becomes dense or concealed.

application_repeatability.application_timing: During overlapping pest generations
application_repeatability.recommendation: Maintain uniform spray coverage to capture both mobile and sessile target stages.

strain_or_active_strength: Combined actives: neem oil 70% plus spinosad 0.8%.
shelf_life_statement: Typical shelf life: 24 months in the unopened original container under cool, dry, shaded storage.
compatibility_statement: Compatible with most neutral-pH spray inputs; avoid strongly alkaline mixes and confirm with a jar test.

problem_solution.problem: The crop has simultaneous sucking pest pressure and larval feeding injury.
problem_solution.solution: Use the combination to obtain both repellency/growth suppression and rapid feeding cessation in a single program step.

problem_solution.problem: Pest recovery occurs between single-mode botanical sprays.
problem_solution.solution: Repeat on a 7-10 day interval to suppress overlapping generations and new hatch.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Combination botanical positioned for mixed pest complexes where a single foliar intervention must cover both sap feeders and early chewing larvae.

product_overview: WeedX is a broad-spectrum, non-selective organic herbicide designed for effective control of a wide range of weeds, mosses, algae, and lichens. When applied, it acts on the outer leaf tissues, causing rapid cell disruption that leads to desiccation, wilting, and eventual plant death. The formulation delivers fast-acting results and is effective under a wide range of environmental conditions. WeedX requires minimal sunlight or heat to perform effectively, making it reliable for diverse field conditions.
key_product_highlights: Glyphosate free, Broad spectrum & Non-selective weed control.
Highly effective to be used in both organic and conventional weed management programs.
High formulation stability allows for better spreading and rapid knockdown.
Achieves total kill within 12 - 72 hours depending on environmental conditions.
Unique mode of action that shuts down photosynthesis and kills only weeds.
Not harmful to beneficial crops when used as directed.

mechanism_of_action.description: Contact-active fatty acids solubilize cuticular lipids and disrupt cell membranes, causing leakage, desiccation, and rapid loss of mobility in exposed pests. Feeding stops soon after contact, egg viability is reduced on treated surfaces, and repeated coverage suppresses population establishment and reproduction.

dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Foliar contact spray with complete wetting
Foliar spray directed at exposed colonies and lower surfaces

application_repeatability.application_timing: At first appearance of exposed colonies
application_repeatability.recommendation: Reapply promptly because residual persistence is short and new contact is needed for control.

application_repeatability.application_timing: During rapid colony expansion
application_repeatability.recommendation: Target exposed insects directly and avoid use under high heat on sensitive foliage.

shelf_life_statement: Typical shelf life: 24 months in the unopened original container under cool, dry, shaded storage.
compatibility_statement: Compatible with most neutral-pH spray inputs; avoid strongly alkaline mixes and confirm with a jar test.

problem_solution.problem: Soft-bodied pests are building quickly on exposed leaf surfaces.
problem_solution.solution: Use as a high-contact spray to disrupt cuticle integrity and quickly stop feeding.

problem_solution.problem: Egg and crawler stages continue to repopulate the crop.
problem_solution.solution: Repeat short-interval sprays to contact fresh hatch and newly established colonies.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Fatty-acid contact botanical positioned for exposed soft-bodied pest stages where fast knockdown and short persistence are acceptable.

product_overview: Admira Adyme is a plant-based botanical bio-insecticide formulated with thyme oil extracted from Thymus species. It contains naturally occurring compounds such as thymol, which provide strong insecticidal, antimicrobial, and repellent properties. It acts primarily through contact and fumigant action, disrupting pests and affecting their respiration and cellular functions. It is effective against a wide range of insect pests and mites in agricultural crops. Due to its natural origin, biodegradability, and low environmental impact, Admira Adyme is well suited for sustainable crop protection and Integrated Pest Management (IPM) programs.
key_product_highlights: Contains natural essential oil with high thymol (36–55%) and carvacrol levels.
Provides broad-spectrum antimicrobial and insecticidal action.
Rapid knockdown and deterrent effect on common insect pests and fungal pathogens.
Biodegradable with minimal environmental impact.
Safe for use in organic and sustainable agriculture systems.

mechanism_of_action.description: Thyme oil constituents such as thymol and related phenolics disrupt cell membranes and alter neurophysiological signaling in exposed insects. The treatment acts as a feeding deterrent, interferes with immature development and oviposition, and suppresses pest establishment on treated foliage.

dilution_dosage_per_acre.crop_stage: Early infestation or preventive canopy spray
dilution_dosage_per_acre.application_method: Foliar spray after preparing a uniform suspension
Foliar spray with attention to lower leaf surfaces and hidden colonies

application_repeatability.application_timing: At first colonization of foliage or flowers
application_repeatability.recommendation: Spray during cooler periods to reduce loss of volatile actives and improve crop safety.

application_repeatability.application_timing: During sustained low to moderate pest pressure
application_repeatability.recommendation: Maintain close spray intervals because persistence is shorter than conventional residual insecticides.

shelf_life_statement: Typical shelf life: 24 months in the unopened original container under cool, dry, shaded storage.
compatibility_statement: Compatible with most neutral-pH spray inputs; avoid strongly alkaline mixes and confirm with a jar test.

problem_solution.problem: Exposed sucking pests are settling on fresh crop growth.
problem_solution.solution: Apply early to repel feeding, disrupt colony establishment, and reduce oviposition.

problem_solution.problem: Volatile-sensitive botanical performance is inconsistent under hot conditions.
problem_solution.solution: Spray during cooler periods and maintain good coverage of new flush.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Thyme oil WP positioned as a volatile botanical for early-stage pest deterrence and contact suppression.

product_overview: Admira Admon is a plant-based botanical bio-insecticide formulated with cinnamon oil extracted from Cinnamomum species. It contains naturally occurring compounds such as cinnamaldehyde, which provide strong insecticidal, repellent, and antimicrobial properties. Admira Admon works primarily through contact and fumigant action, disrupting pest activity and affecting their respiration and cellular functions. It is effective against a wide range of insect pests, mites, and certain fungal pathogens in agricultural and horticultural crops. Due to its natural origin, biodegradability, and low environmental impact, it is well suited for sustainable crop protection and Integrated Pest Management (IPM) programs.
key_product_highlights: Botanical active ingredient — suitable for reduced-risk/eco-aware programs.
Broad-spectrum activity against a range of chewing and sucking insects, mites, and certain fungal pathogens.
Fast knockdown and repellency for many target pests.
Low residue profile when used as directed.
Compatible with many IPM strategies; may reduce reliance on synthetic chemistries.

mechanism_of_action.description: Cinnamon oil constituents, especially cinnamaldehyde-rich fractions, disrupt membrane integrity and respiratory metabolism in exposed insects. The treatment deters feeding, disturbs developmental physiology in immature stages, and suppresses oviposition and egg viability on treated crop surfaces.

problem_solution.problem: Soft-bodied pests are colonizing young foliage and floral structures.
problem_solution.solution: Use directed foliar sprays to reduce settlement, feeding, and egg laying.

problem_solution.solution: Repeat short-interval sprays to sustain contact and reproductive suppression.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.

product_overview: Admira Adrlic is a plant-based botanical bio-insecticide formulated with garlic oil extracted from Allium sativum. It contains naturally occurring sulfur compounds such as allicin, known for their insecticidal, repellent, and antimicrobial properties. Admira Adrlic works primarily through contact and repellent action, disrupting pest feeding and activity. It is effective against a wide range of insect pests and mites in agricultural and horticultural crops. Due to its natural origin, biodegradability, and low environmental impact, it supports sustainable crop protection and Integrated Pest Management (IPM) programs.
key_product_highlights: Natural botanical repellent with low mammalian toxicity.
Broad-spectrum control with contact and vapor-phase action.
Safe for pollinators and beneficial insects when used correctly.
Fast biodegradation and residue-free produce.
Ideal for IPM and organic farming programs.

mechanism_of_action.description: Garlic oil organosulfur compounds act as feeding deterrents and volatile repellents while also disturbing respiratory and membrane-associated processes in exposed insects. This reduces feeding intensity, impairs immature development, and suppresses adult settling and oviposition on treated surfaces.

problem_solution.problem: Repeated pest settling is occurring on fresh flush after irrigation or pruning.
problem_solution.solution: Use garlic oil to create a repellent canopy barrier and reduce feeding initiation.

problem_solution.problem: Low-level sucking pest pressure persists despite sanitation and scouting.
problem_solution.solution: Apply repeated botanical sprays to disrupt settlement and reproduction.

storage_safety: Store sealed in the original pack away from heat, direct sunlight, and moisture.
technical_positioning_summary: Garlic oil WP positioned for repellent canopy protection and early-stage suppression in botanical spray programs.

product_overview: Admira Adove is a plant-based botanical bio-insecticide formulated with clove oil extracted from Syzygium aromaticum. It contains naturally occurring compounds such as eugenol, known for their insecticidal, repellent, and antimicrobial properties. Admira Adove acts primarily through contact action, disrupting pest cell membranes and physiological processes. It is effective against a broad range of insect pests and mites in agricultural and horticultural crops. Due to its natural origin, biodegradability, and low environmental impact, it is suitable for sustainable crop protection and Integrated Pest Management (IPM) programs.
key_product_highlights: Natural alternative to synthetic fungicides and insecticides
Broad-spectrum antimicrobial and insecticidal activity
Enhances plant health by reducing disease and pest pressure
Safe for beneficial insects and non-target organisms when used as directed
Compatible with most agrochemicals and fertilizers

mechanism_of_action.description: Clove oil constituents dominated by eugenol disrupt membrane function and neurophysiological signaling in exposed insects. The treatment acts as a feeding deterrent, interferes with immature development and oviposition, and reduces pest survival on treated plant surfaces.
